ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'Jackie Chun' — the canonical tournament alias of Master Roshi. The alias is explicitly named in Dragon Ball lore as Roshi's disguise identity used in the 21st World Martial Arts Tournament. Art matches: white-haired elder in white gi with electric energy, consistent with Roshi/Jackie Chun's canonical tournament appearance.

trivia_bullets.text: Jackie Chun is the tournament alias Master Roshi adopted by gluing on a white hair toupee and removing his glasses. He entered the 21st World Martial Arts Tournament under this name specifically so his students would not know they were fighting him. [CBR: How Master Roshi Got the Nickname Jackie Chun]

Roshi's stated rationale for the disguise: if his students Goku and Krillin won the tournament easily, they might believe themselves the strongest in the world and stop training. By entering as a formidable unknown opponent, Roshi ensured they would face defeat and remain driven. Corrupted-stewardship-by-mask is the operative register: mentorship exercised through deliberate concealment. [CBR: How Master Roshi Got the Nickname Jackie Chun]

This card is a Super Rare (TB2-058) in the DBS-TB02 World Martial Arts Tournament set released September 21, 2018, placing it among the highest-rarity pulls in the themed booster — appropriate for the tournament's iconic disguised elder. [dbs-cardgame.com]

Roshi and Master Shen (Crane School) trained together under the legendary Master Mutaito, making the Jackie Chun / Master Shen pairing within this same set a canonical rivalry between two students of the same lineage who chose opposite philosophical paths. [Dragon Ball Wiki: Master Shen, via WebSearch snippet]

related_cards.name: Master Shen, Martial Meister
related_cards.note: Roshi and Shen share the same origin master (Mutaito); rival school dynamic.

related_cards.name: Trusting Relationship Kami
related_cards.note: Both Kami and Roshi/Jackie Chun are canonical elder-stewardship figures in the same set.
